title: sigesbeckia orientalis subsp. glabrescens
intro: Sigesbeckia orientalis subsp. glabrescens, also known as Japanese Mugwort or Chinese Wormwood, is a subspecies of the Sigesbeckia orientalis plant, a type of flowering plant native to Asia. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antiviral properties. The health benefits of this plant include reducing fever, relieving pain and inflammation, and improving respiratory health. Sigesbeckia orientalis subsp. glabrescens can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is sometimes used in traditional Chinese medicine.
